title: 10 best herbal teas for hyperthyroidism
intro_desc: Herbal teas for hyperthyroidism are a natural and soothing way to alleviate symptoms of an overactive thyroid gland. These teas work by calming the body's metabolic rate, reducing anxiety and stress, and promoting relaxation. Examples include peppermint tea, which helps to calm the digestive system, and chamomile tea, which soothes nerves and promotes sleep. Drinking these teas regularly can improve lives by reducing symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and fatigue, allowing individuals to feel more balanced and comfortable in their own bodies.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha teas helps with hyperthyroidism because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can exacerbate thyroid issues. The adaptogenic properties of ashwagandha also help to regulate cortisol levels, which can become imbalanced in individuals with hyperthyroidism. Additionally, ashwagandha's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, common symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism. By promoting relaxation and balancing thyroid function, ashwagandha teas can provide a natural and calming solution for those managing hyperthyroidism.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ashwagandha tea helps to balance cortisol levels, reducing the negative effects of stress on thyroid function and alleviating symptoms of hyperthyroidism.

Antioxidant properties: Ashwagandha's antioxidants combat free radicals that can damage thyroid tissue and disrupt its functioning, which is particularly important in hyperthyroidism where inflammation is often present.

Thyroid-regulating properties: Ashwagandha tea has been shown to regulate thyroid hormone production and secretion, helping to reduce the excessive production of thyroid hormones that occurs in hyperthyroidism.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, ashwagandha roots are effective in reducing cortisol levels and anxiety, which can help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.

Fruits: The fruits of the ashwagandha plant contain a unique combination of flavonoids and saponins that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them useful for managing hyperthyroidism-related inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gently boil one teaspoon of dried ashwagandha root powder in two cups of water for five minutes.
Steep the boiled mixture for an additional three minutes before straining it into a cup.
Discard the solids and let the tea cool down to room temperature within 30 minutes.
Mix equal parts of the cooled tea with freshly squeezed juice from one lemon slice within 60 minutes.
Consume the tea-lemon mixture twice daily, 30 minutes before breakfast and dinner, for a minimum period of six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with hyperthyroidism because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland. This anti-inflammatory effect can help alleviate symptoms such as rapid heartbeat and weight loss associated with an overactive thyroid. Additionally, licorice tea may also help regulate thyroid hormone production by inhibiting the conversion of T4 to T3, which can lead to a reduction in thyrotoxicosis. Overall, herbal licorice teas have been traditionally used to support thyroid health and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can exacerbate thyroid issues by calming the immune system and reducing inflammatory responses.
Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants, scavenging free radicals and protecting against damage to thyroid tissue, which can occur due to hyperthyroidism-induced oxidative stress.

Glycyrrhizic acid: Inhibits the production of a hormone called 5-lipoxygenase, which is involved in inflammation and immune responses, potentially reducing thyroid hormone production and alleviating hyperthyroid symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps regulate thyroid hormone production and reduces symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
Rhyzomes: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Gather required ingredients including dried licorice root 2-3 teaspoons per cup boiling water.
Measure and add one teaspoon of dried licorice root for every cup of cold water.
Heat filtered water in a saucepan until it reaches boiling point then remove from heat.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or longer for maximum extraction of medicinal properties.
Strain the tea into cups and consume immediately while warm to alleviate symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with hyperthyroidism because it acts as a natural diuretic, helping to flush out excess thyroxine (T4) and triiodothyronine (T3) hormones from the body. The tea's bitter compounds also support liver function, which can help metabolize excess thyroid hormones. Additionally, dandelion roots contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant properties, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with hyperthyroidism. By supporting hormone balance and detoxification, dandelion teas may alleviate symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and anxiety.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and triterpenoids, can help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland and surrounding tissues, which is often exacerbated in cases of hyperthyroidism.

Liver support: Dandelion tea has been shown to stimulate liver function, which may help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism by facilitating the removal of excess thyroid hormones from the bloodstream.

Thyroid hormone regulation: The flavonoids and triterpenoids present in dandelion tea may have a regulating effect on thyroid hormone production, helping to normalize thyroid function and reduce symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism, such as anxiety, tremors, and rapid heartbeat.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with hyperthyroidism.
Rhyzomes: Dandelion rhyzomes contain a unique compound called inulin, which has been shown to regulate thyroid hormone levels and improve symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers measuring one teaspoon per cup of boiling water.
Measure two cups of filtered water in a medium-sized saucepan and bring it to a rolling boil for 5 minutes.
Add the measured taraxacum officinale mixture to the boiling water and reduce heat to low simmering for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth into a large ceramic mug holding one cup of tea.
Let the tea steep for an additional 5 minutes before serving warm or at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maca teas helps with hyperthyroidism because it supports thyroid function by balancing hormonal levels. The plant's adaptogenic properties help to stabilize the body's response to stress, which can contribute to an overactive thyroid gland. Additionally, maca tea has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the thyroid gland, which can alleviate symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and anxiety associated with hyperthyroidism. By promoting a healthy thyroid function, maca tea may help to regulate metabolism and alleviate related symptoms.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Maca tea helps to balance the body's response to stress, reducing symptoms of anxiety and fatigue associated with hyperthyroidism.
Antioxidant properties: Maca tea's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage thyroid tissue, potentially slowing or reversing thyroid damage caused by hyperthyroidism.
Thyroid-regulating properties: Maca tea may help regulate thyroid hormone production, reducing symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as rapid heartbeat and weight loss.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, maca roots help regulate thyroid hormones and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
Rhyzomes: Containing a high concentration of glucosinolates, rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the thyroid gland and reduce inflammation associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried lepidium meyenii root powder and 2 cups of boiling water.
Steep the powder in hot water for 15 minutes to extract its active compounds slowly.
Strain the tea into a cup using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve carefully.
Add one tablespoon of honey as sweetener and mix well with a spoon thoroughly.
Allow the tea to cool down for at least 10 minutes before consumption safely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with hyperthyroidism because its unique composition of compounds, including flavonoids and polyphenols, has been shown to possess natural thyroid-regulating properties. The tea's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects may help alleviate symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and anxiety commonly associated with hyperthyroidism. Additionally, the tea's ability to stimulate metabolism without over-stimulating the thyroid gland makes it a promising natural remedy for managing this condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate thyroid inflammation and oxidative stress, which contribute to hyperthyroidism.

Thyroid-regulating effects: The flavonoids present in Ginkgo biloba tea may help regulate thyroid function by inhibiting the conversion of thyroxine (T4) to triiodothyronine (T3), thus reducing excessive thyroid activity.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which is often a hallmark of hyperthyroidism, and alleviate related symptoms such as heat intolerance and joint pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help regulate thyroid hormone levels and reduce symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism.
Rhyzomes: Containing anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, rhyzomes help soothe and calm the body's metabolic processes, alleviating hyperthyroidism symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a trusted source and wash them thoroughly with cold water.
Measure out one teaspoon of crushed ginkgo biloba leaves per cup of boiling filtered water.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 10 minutes in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup to allow flavors to meld.
Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids.
Serve hot or chilled, enjoying the subtle herbal flavor and potential therapeutic benefits within.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea teas helps with hyperthyroidism because its natural compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to have a calming effect on the thyroid gland. The anti-inflammatory properties of herbal tea can help reduce inflammation in the body, which is often associated with hyperthyroidism. Additionally, herbal tea's ability to regulate blood sugar levels and improve digestion can also contribute to its benefits in managing hyperthyroidism symptoms, such as weight loss and anxiety. By incorporating herbal tea into one's daily routine, individuals may experience a reduction in thyroid hormone production and improved overall health.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic compounds: Herbal teas like Ashwagandha, Ginseng, and Rhodiola rosea contain adaptogenic compounds that help the body adapt to stress and normalize thyroid function by reducing cortisol levels and promoting relaxation.

Antioxidant properties: Teas like Green tea, Turmeric, and Ginger are rich in antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism symptoms such as fatigue, weight loss, and anxiety.

Thyroid-regulating compounds: Herbal teas like Bladderwrack, Kelp, and Licorice root contain iodine-rich compounds that may help regulate thyroid function by supporting the production of thyroid hormones and reducing inflammation in the thyroid gland.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the tea plant have been traditionally used to reduce thyroid hormone levels, making them a natural remedy for hyperthyroidism.
Barks: The barks of the tea plant contain compounds that help regulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried green tea leaves and slice them into small pieces to prepare for brewing.
Boil filtered water in a teapot or kettle and pour it over one teaspoon of sliced green tea leaves.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, releasing flavonoids and antioxidants.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or tea infuser, discarding solids.
Drink the prepared tea immediately, enjoying its soothing and calming effects within 10-15 minutes after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with hyperthyroidism because it has a calming effect on the body, which can help to reduce symptoms such as anxiety, jitteriness, and rapid heartbeat. The menthol in peppermint also has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help to ease thyroid inflammation and promote overall relaxation. Additionally, peppermint tea's cooling sensation can help to soothe an overactive thyroid gland, promoting a sense of calmness and balance throughout the body.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant Properties: Peppermint tea helps to soothe digestive issues, such as nausea and vomiting, which are common symptoms of hyperthyroidism, by stimulating digestive enzymes and relaxing the muscles in the stomach.

Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: The menthol content in peppermint tea has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation caused by thyroid hormone imbalances, which can alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, muscle weakness, and heat intolerance.

Calming and Sedative Effects: Peppermint tea's calming and sedative effects can help to reduce anxiety and stress levels, which are often exacerbated by hyperthyroidism, promoting better sleep quality and overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, peppermint roots help reduce thyroid inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism.

Barks: The bark of the peppermint plant contains flavonoids that have been shown to exhibit a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and stress associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the peppermint leaves in the boiling water for seven to ten minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup to remove the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking it immediately after preparation.
Drink one to two cups of peppermint tea three times a day as needed for symptom relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm teas helps with hyperthyroidism because of its unique ability to soothe and calm the digestive system. Hyperthyroidism causes inflammation and irritation in the gut, which can further exacerbate thyroid issues. The mucilages present in slippery elm tea coat and protect the mucous membranes in the stomach and intestines, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria. This helps to alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea often associated with hyperthyroidism, allowing the body to focus on healing and regulating thyroid function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Slippery elm's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as mucilages, can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the thyroid gland, which is often associated with hyperthyroidism.

Thyroid-protective properties: Slippery elm's antioxidants and flavonoids may help protect the thyroid gland from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism.

Adaptogenic and calming effects: Slippery elm has been traditionally used as an adaptogen to calm the nervous system and reduce stress, which is often exacerbated by hyperthyroidism; its calming effects may help regulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of slippery elm contain mucilages that help soothe and calm the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as diarrhea.
Barks: The barks of slippery elm have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, a common symptom of hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried ulmus rubra bark and prepare 2 teaspoons of it for brewing.
Boil fresh water in a pot and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes to create a decoction.
Combine the prepared ulmus rubra bark with the boiling water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh strainer and discard the solids.
Drink the cooled tea immediately, consuming 2-3 cups per day as needed for hyperthyroidism relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto teas helps with hyperthyroidism because it has been shown to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), which can exacerbate thyroid hormone production. By blocking DHT, saw palmetto tea may help reduce symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and anxiety often associated with hyperthyroidism. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe inflammation in the thyroid gland, further alleviating symptoms of this condition. Regular consumption of saw palmetto tea may provide relief for those suffering from hyperthyroidism.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Saw palmetto's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and fatty acids, may help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism symptoms.

5-alpha-reductase inhibition: Saw palmetto's active compound, beta-sitosterol, has been shown to inhibit 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that converts testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can exacerbate thyroid issues and hyperthyroidism symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: Saw palmetto tea's antioxidant compounds may help protect the thyroid gland from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism development.
remedy_parts: Roots: Saw palmetto roots are rich in fatty acids and phytosterols, which help reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
Seeds: Saw palmetto seeds contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help regulate thyroid function and reduce oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried serenoa repens herb in the amount of one teaspoon per eight ounces of water.
Heat filtered water to boiling point and let it cool for three to five minutes before pouring.
Add the dried serenoa repens herb to a teapot or infuser and pour the heated water over it.
Allow the mixture to steep for ten to fifteen minutes before straining and discarding the solids.
Enjoy the tea hot or chilled, taking one to three cups per day as needed for hyperthyroidism relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guggul teas helps with hyperthyroidism because it contains natural compounds that help regulate thyroid hormones. The tea's active ingredient, guggulu resin, has been shown to decrease levels of thyroxine (T4) and triiodothyronine (T3), which are abnormally high in individuals with hyperthyroidism. Additionally, guggul teas have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, promoting a healthier balance of hormones and alleviating symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and anxiety.
remedy_properties: Guggulsterones: These bioactive compounds have been shown to reduce thyroid hormone production by inhibiting the conversion of thyroxine (T4) to triiodothyronine (T3), thereby helping to alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Guggul teas have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism-related symptoms such as fatigue and weight loss.

Thyroid-regulating activity: The saponins present in guggul tea have been shown to have a regulatory effect on thyroid function, helping to normalize an overactive thyroid gland (hyperthyroidism) and reduce the risk of complications such as osteoporosis and cardiovascular disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in guggulusterones, which help reduce thyroid hormone levels and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
Rhyzomes: Contain a combination of glycosides and resinoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in managing hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried commiphora mukul bark into a heat-resistant cup.
Bring eight ounces of water to boiling point in a kettle or pot.
Pour the boiling water over the dried commiphora mukul bark in the cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on desired strength and flavor.
Strain the tea into another cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hyperthyroidism with herbal teas is by incorporating calming and cooling herbs into your daily routine. Peppermint tea, for example, helps to soothe the nervous system and reduce anxiety symptoms common in hyperthyroid patients. Lemon balm tea also exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate thyroid-related inflammation. Additionally, licorice root tea has been shown to inhibit the production of thyroid hormones, providing relief from hyperthyroidism symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hyperthyroidism that are treated with herbal teas are adrenal fatigue, thyroiditis, and Hashimoto's disease. Herbs like ashwagandha, guggulu, and licorice root are commonly used in teas to help alleviate symptoms such as anxiety, insomnia, weight loss, and rapid heart rate. These herbs work by supporting the adrenal glands and regulating cortisol levels, helping to reduce stress and promote overall thyroid health.
